steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The structure is: main clause with opening em-dash + incomplete appositive (a strong and flexible) + blank + infinitive phrase (craft baskets). The blank must complete the appositive noun and connect it to the infinitive.

steps.title: Apply the appositive punctuation rule
steps.content: When an appositive interrupts the main clause, a matching em-dash must enclose it without breaking the sentence. Material—to closes the appositive with an em-dash and flows into the infinitive phrase to craft baskets. Choices A and B break the sentence with periods, creating a fragment; C's semicolon incorrectly requires independent clauses. That matches choice D — D is correct.
